mnemonics: CPU Time = IC * CPI * Clock Cycle Time (Think: 'I Count Cycles Per Clock')
keyPoints: CPU execution time is the true measure of computer performance.
Instructions per second (IPS) and clock rate can be misleading if not considered with CPI and instruction count.
Amdahl's Law states that the performance improvement is limited by the fraction of time the enhanced feature is used.
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock rate (GHz) always means better performance, ignoring CPI and IC.
Confusing CPU time (the true measure) with clock cycle time or MIPS.
Misapplying Amdahl's Law by not correctly identifying the fraction of time improved.
